approach: revise the existing doctrine/plan-retirement node — additive scenarios over a frozen suite, no new node
extend the sweep's file set from the plan pair to the full transient CR artifact set
reuse the existing mechanical distilled signal rather than adding a second ledger field
engine change is test-covered and PR-reviewed; the deletion stays caller-cleared and fail-closed

why: SQ-F4: codify the transient CR artifact set + extend the retirement sweep to it. 6 additive scenarios on doctrine/plan-retirement (structural gherkin-cli diff addOnly true, 0 modified/removed, so the suite self-clears and stays @frozen, no re-open); the set codified in design/provenance-model.md. Design settled at explore: the distilled gate is UNCHANGED (cleared AND present(plan.md) AND (distilled OR !logPresent)) and only the deleted file set widens. Briefs neither widen the gate (only the combat log owes a distillation; gating on a brief would re-strand the no-log mission class) nor anchor presence (plan.md alone does; widening it would have narrowed the frozen no-plan-is-a-no-op scenario and a filesystem check confirmed zero existing orphans, so it would buy nothing). Both boundary calls user-confirmed at the grill. Round-1 cold spec-judge FAILED oracle+builder: the briefs-do-not-anchor-presence use case had no covering scenario (an impl treating a brief as an alternate presence signal would have passed the whole suite) plus two Thens asserting rationale not filesystem-observables; both fixed, corrections logged. Round-2 cold spec-judge ALIGNED true (oracle/builder/architect pass), no blockers, no open markers, and confirmed the new no-op scenario genuinely discriminates. Architect observation on two stale parent index rows (doctrine/README, plugin/README still naming only the plan pair) fixed pre-gate rather than deferred, as stale prose vs shipped behavior is this project's own prose-impl-contradiction cause. Self-asserted within auto-all leash; low blast radius, PR-reviewed, deletion stays caller-cleared and fail-closed.

Cold sdd-impl-judge IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true — all 17 frozen scenarios verified against an independently re-derived oracle (no scenario-bridge present, so each judged by hand), producer suite 36/36, root pnpm verify green 21/21. Judge confirmed by git-diff that the retirement decision is byte-identical pre/post-CR (decideRetirements, distilledCrRefs, discoverPlans, discoverLogs untouched) — the only functional change is transientArtifactFiles returning 5 names instead of 2, so regression in the 11 pre-existing scenarios is structurally impossible. It authored 3 independent adversarial probes beyond the producer's tests and confirmed both boundary invariants genuinely discriminate: presence stays anchored on plan.md even when a real combat log exists for the ref, briefs never enter logPresent, and exact-suffix concatenation defeats prefix collision in both directions. Deletion confirmed tree-only (unlinkSync, no git subprocess). No hollow tests, no dead exports, no prose-impl drift in SKILL.md's node-absent fallback. Self-asserted within auto-all leash. Project status stays approved and no standing approval.impl is written: this side-quest does not make the SDD project as a whole implemented, and the durable per-CR record is this line.
